key_insight: RetiBrain uses retinal fundus photos to predict MRI-derived brain health biomarkers, linking eye images to neurodegeneration- and cerebrovascular-related risk.
clinical_relevance: If validated broadly, this low-cost, scalable imaging approach could support population screening and longitudinal monitoring of brain health without MRI.
method: Cross-modal deep learning distills MRI-based latent representations into a retinal image model.
Predicts six WMH- and hippocampal-volume biomarkers from color fundus photography.
Adds interpretable retinal structure analyses to connect image features with brain injury patterns.
evidence_signal: In a paired CFP-MRI cohort, the model improved mean Pearson correlation from 0.240 to 0.549 versus RETFound and reached 0.640 for periventricular WMH prediction; in a longitudinal cohort it predicted dementia risk with AUROC 0.824.
watch_item: Results come from retrospective cohorts, so external validation and assessment of fairness across devices, sites, and populations are still needed.
paper_title: Retina-derived Quantitative Biomarkers of Brain Health
